hypothesis: Continual pre-training of a large language model on MOF-related literature and PubChem data, followed by supervised fine-tuning using MOFid text representations on MOF property prediction tasks (band gap and gas adsorption), will yield lower mean absolute error on held-out test sets than traditional graph-based baselines (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer), and this advantage will be amplified under low-resource fine-tuning data regimes (1K, 3K, 10K samples).
